events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need assess heat trace industrial site, rough-complete, observed condition: no GFPE breaker. Reference NEC 427. Need determine decision fail because non-compliant/dangerous? Need know NEC 427 requirements for ground-fault protection of equipment? NEC 427.22? Let's recall NEC Article 427: Fixed Electric Heating for Pipelines and Vessels. Section 427.22 Ground-Fault Protection of Equipment. Each branch circuit supplying heat trace must be provided with ground-fault protection of equipment (GFPE) if required? Let's recall exact: 427.22 Ground-Fault Protection of Equipment. Each branch circuit supplying heat-tracing equipment shall be provided with ground-fault protection of equipment. Unless? Maybe exceptions for single conductor? Let's verify. NEC 427.22: Each branch circuit supplying heat-tracing equipment shall be provided with ground-fault protection of equipment. This is to prevent fires from damaged heating cables. So no GFPE breaker is a defect, non-compliant. Work rough-complete. Decision fail. Risk high? Missing GFPE is shock/fire hazard. Component conditions installed-defective, non-compliant. Percent complete roughly 70% until defects corrected, as scenario says. Value 0. Sound_source etc default empty string/0. Workable empty. Findings: no GFPE breaker for heat trace branch circuit. Actions: install GFPE breaker per NEC 427.22, verify settings, retest. Rationale: NEC requires GFPE for each heat trace branch circuit; absence leaves fault current unprotected and violates code. References NEC 427.22. Need ensure JSON shape. component_conditions list includes installed-defective and non-compliant. percent_complete 70. value 0. sound_source ""? Schema says string. event_time 0.0, rate 0.0, order list, workable list. confidence maybe 0.95. Need include all fields.

fail","risk":"high","work_stage":"rough-complete","component_conditions":["installed-defective","non-compliant"],"percent_complete":70,"value":0,"sound_source":"","confidence":0.95,"event_time":0.0,"rate":0.0,"order":[],"workable":[],"findings":["Heat trace branch circuit is missing required ground-fault protection of equipment (GFPE) breaker","Installation is non-compliant with NEC Article 427 and presents fire/shock hazard if energized"],"actions":["Install listed GFPE breaker on each heat trace branch circuit per NEC 427.22","Verify GFPE pickup settings coordinate with heat trace cable manufacturer requirements","Test/reset GFPE function and document before energizing or closing out"],"rationale":"NEC 427 requires GFPE for each branch circuit supplying heat-tracing equipment. A roughed-in heat trace circuit without GFPE is defective and cannot be accepted or energized; overall completion is held at roughly 70% until the protective device is installed and verified.","references":["NEC 427.22 Ground-Fault Protection of Equipment"]}
